title: cystostemon hispidus
intro: Cystostemon hispidus, also known as the Sticky Bells, is a plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its distinctive sticky, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, due to its antispasmodic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Cystostemon hispidus include relieving coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as soothing digestive issues such as dyspepsia and diarrhea. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, making it a versatile herbal remedy for various health concerns.
